race.name: Biela stopa
race.tagline: Fifty kilometers across the Kremnica highlands at 1,200 meters — Slovakia's oldest cross-country race, where Central European grit meets mountain solitude.

vitals.aid_stations: 3 feed stations with warm tea, energy food, and Slovak pastries

climate.description: February in the Kremnica Mountains at 860-1,230m altitude brings cold continental conditions. Temperatures range from -12°C to -2°C. Snow is generally reliable at altitude but the mid-mountain location can experience freeze-thaw cycles. Wind exposure on ridge sections adds chill factor.
challenges: Variable snow conditions at mid-mountain altitude
Wind exposure on highland ridge sections
Freeze-thaw cycles can create icy tracks
Limited infrastructure in remote mountain sections

course.primary: A highland course through the Kremnica Mountains between 860m and 1,230m altitude. The terrain is rolling with moderate climbs through mixed spruce and beech forests. The course traverses the Skalka plateau with views across the central Slovak highlands. A genuine mountain marathon that tests fitness and resilience.
course.surface: Machine-groomed tracks through mountain forest and highland plateau
course.grooming: Solid grooming on established trails, weather-dependent in exposed sections
features: Slovakia's oldest cross-country ski race — over 50 years of history
Views across the Kremnica Mountains and central Slovak highlands
Authentic Central European mountain skiing atmosphere

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: Solid mid-range scores across the board reflect a well-established but regional event. Over 50 years of history and Euroloppet membership provide credibility, but limited international draw and variable snow conditions constrain the overall score.

history.summary: Founded around 1973, Biela Stopa ('White Track') is Slovakia's oldest cross-country ski event. Set in the Kremnica Mountains of central Slovakia, the race has endured through the Czechoslovak era, the Velvet Divorce, and EU accession, maintaining its identity as an authentic Central European mountain marathon. Joining the Euroloppet series in 1994 brought international recognition to this storied event.
notable_facts: Slovakia's oldest cross-country ski event — founded circa 1973
Survived the transition from Czechoslovakia to independent Slovakia
Kremnica is historically famous for its medieval gold mint
Course traverses the Skalka plateau above the historic mining town
